revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''大和国風土記'''（やまとのくに ふどき）は、[[奈良時代]]に編纂された[[大和国]]の[[風土記]]。 現存する記述は...」
revision.text:
'''大和国風土記'''（やまとのくに ふどき）は、[[奈良時代]]に編纂された[[大和国]]の[[風土記]]。
現存する記述は、[[逸文]]という形でのみ伝わる（以下、逸文の記述）。
== 三都嫁 == 大和国の風土記に云わく、[[天津神]]命、石津神命、三都嫁（みとのまぐわい<ref>男女の交合の意。『風土記 [[日本古典文学大系]]2』 [[岩波書店]] 14刷1971年（1刷1958年） p.421の脚注。</ref>）、遊（うらぶれ・物思いする<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）、面語（おもがたり・面と向き合って語る）してとあり。
『毘沙門堂本古今集註』からの逸文（『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』）。
== 大口真神原 == むかし明日香の地に老狼ありて多くの人を食らう。土民畏れて[[真神|大口の真神]]という。その住める所を名付けて、大口の真神原という云々風土記に見えたり。
[[飛鳥大仏]]辺りの古称で『[[万葉集]]』にも見える<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>。
『枕詞燭明抄（中）』からの逸文（『風土記 日本古典文学大家2』）。
== 御杖神宮 == 風土記に曰く、[[宇陀郡|宇陀の郡]]、篠幡（ささはた）の庄（現秦原町山辺三の篠畑<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）、御杖の神の宮。祭れる正魂霊（おおみたま）にあらず（[[天照大神]]の御魂を祭っているのではないの意<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）。[[倭比賣命]]、天照大神を戴（いただ）き、御杖となりて、この地（ところ）に至りき。よりて御宮地をたずねて三月を経（へ）、終に神戸（[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神領]]の民戸<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）となしき。
『[[日本書紀]]』通證十一からの逸文（『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』）。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまとのくにふとき}} [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:風土記]] [[Category:逸書]]

[[飛鳥大仏]]辺りの古称で『[[万葉集]]』にも見える<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>。

== 御杖神宮 == 風土記に曰く、[[宇陀郡|宇陀の郡]]、篠幡（ささはた）の庄（現秦原町山辺三の篠畑<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）、御杖の神の宮。祭れる正魂霊（おおみたま）にあらず（[[天照大神]]の御魂を祭っているのではないの意<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）。[[倭姫命|倭比賣命]]、天照大神を戴（いただ）き、御杖となりて、この地（ところ）に至りき。よりて御宮地をたずねて三月を経（へ）、終に神戸（[[伊勢神宮]]の[[神領]]の民戸<ref>『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』 p.421の脚注。</ref>）となしき。
『[[日本書紀]]』通證十一からの逸文（『風土記 日本古典文学大系2』）。

{{DEFAULTSORT:やまとのくにふとき}} [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:風土記]] [[Category:逸書]]

revision.text:
'''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki''' (大和国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, compiled during the [[Nara period]]. The text survives only in the form of {{ill|fragments|ja|逸文}}, as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' [[Iwanami Shoten]] Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇陀郡}}, in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Notes == === Annotations === {{reflist|2|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}}

They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}

The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

[[ja:大和国風土記]]

'''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki''' (大和国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, compiled during the [[Nara period]]. The text survives only in the form of {{ill|fragments|ja|逸文}}, as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' [[Iwanami Shoten]] Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇陀郡}}, in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Notes == === Annotations === {{reflist|2|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}}

They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}

The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

The text survives only in the form of [[:en:Lost literary work|fragments]], as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]] Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|qq=|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

The text survives only in the form of [[:en:Lost literary work|fragments]], as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]] Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

The text survives only in the form of [[:en:Lost literary work|fragments]], as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]] Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

The text survives only in the form of [[:en:Lost literary work|fragments]], as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]] Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]].</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Notes == === Annotations === {{reflist|2|group="Note"}} [[ja:大和国風土記]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和国風土記]][[zh:大和國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和国風土記]][[zh:大和國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和国風土記]][[zh:大和國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q27027223]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki''' (Yamato Province 風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, compiled during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. The text survives only in the form of {{ill|Lost literary work|en|Lost literary work|WD=Q1585442|lt=fragments}}, as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}} Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}.</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Notes == === Annotations === {{reflist|2|group="Note"}} [[ja:大和国風土記]]

== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

'''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki''' (Yamato Province 風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, compiled during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. The text survives only in the form of {{ill|Lost literary work|en|Lost literary work|WD=Q1585442|lt=fragments}}, as outlined below.
== Mitonomaguwai (Three Union Marriage) == {{Quotation| The ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' states: "The {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神}} no Mikoto and {{ill|Ishizukami|ja|石津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} no Mikoto engaged in a union known as 'Mitonomaguwai'{{refnest|group="Note"|Referring to the union of man and woman.<ref name="taikeip421">''Fudoki,'' {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}} Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, 14th edition, 1971 (1st edition, 1958), p.421, footnote.</ref>}}. They wandered in thought (''urabure''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'lost in thought.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}} and conversed face-to-face (''omogatari''){{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning 'direct conversation.'<ref name="taikeip421" />}}." | ''Bishamondōbon Kokinshūchū''<ref name="taikei">''Fudoki,'' Japanese Classical Literature Series 2, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}.</ref>}}
== Ōkuchi no Magamikohara == {{Quotation| Long ago, in the land of Asuka, there was an old wolf that devoured many people. The local villagers feared it and called it {{ill|Ōkuchi no Magami|ja|大口真神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Magami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=真神}} ("Great-Mouthed True Deity"). The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}
== Mitsue Jingū == {{Quotation| The Fudoki states: "In the {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, in the village of Sasahata{{refnest|group="Note"|Now the Sasabata area of Hara Town, Yamabe 3.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}, stands the shrine of {{ill|Mitsue no Kami|ja|御杖の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}

The place where it lived was named Ōkuchi no Magamikohara{{refnest|group="Note"|An ancient name for the area near {{ill|Asuka Daibutsu|ja|飛鳥大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Daibutsu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=飛鳥寺|1=Asuka-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779755}}, also mentioned in the ''Man'yōshū.''<ref name="taikeip421" />}} as recorded in the Fudoki. | ''Makurakotoba Shokumeishō (Middle)''<ref name="taikei" />}}

It does not enshrine the Great Spirit{{refnest|group="Note"|Meaning it does not enshrine the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}.<ref name="taikeip421" />}}. {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} carried Amaterasu on her head and, with her as the staff, reached this place. After searching for the shrine's location for three months, she finally established the divine fields as {{ill|Kanjin|ja|神戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|1=Kobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589706}} (lands belonging to the shrine)." | ''Nihon Shoki Tsūshō, Volume 11''<ref name="taikei" />}}
